summary: The test suite only covers three happy-path semantic version bumping scenarios (patch, minor, major) and validates workflow structure. Critical gaps: (1) No tests for package.json version parsing, only VERSION files; (2) No error handling verification despite implementation supporting graceful errors; (3) No changelog content assertions, only artifact existence; (4) No edge cases (invalid semver, whitespace, empty commits, malformed JSON); (5) The 'none' case (no version-impacting commits) is untested; (6) Tests depend on a single `act push` execution—all tests fail if setup fails, violating test independence; (7) Tests grep for output markers (SVB_CASE=patch SVB_NEW_VERSION=1.0.1) that are not shown in the provided workflow file, suggesting workflow may not emit expected output; (8) Referenced fixture files (case_patch_commits.txt, case_minor_commits.txt, case_major_commits.txt) are not provided, making it impossible to verify fixture adequacy or confirm tests would actually run. The workflow file itself (.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml) is also not provided despite being central to the test design. Tests would fail to catch version parsing failures, changelog generation failures, and error-case regressions. Structure tests are reasonable, but coverage is too narrow and rigor insufficient for regression detection.
